memory.summary: I'm way too stressed to start this huge project right now, so I'll definitely grab some leisure time first.

desires: Seek immediate social validation and connection with cohort_3 to relieve current stress
Avoid the looming friction of the term project deadline by delaying action until tomorrow
Maintain a low-conflict, relaxed atmosphere rather than tackling hard tasks alone
Prioritize leisure or casual interaction over academic progress right now

goals.description: Immediately join cohort_3 for a casual hangout or chat to relieve stress and feel connected.
goals.rationale: My highest value is relationships (0.5). I am currently stressed and impulsive, so seeking immediate social validation from my close friends is the only thing that matters right now. Avoiding friction with the looming deadline by distracting myself fits my avoidant nature.

goals.description: Do the quick_chore only if it happens naturally while hanging out with cohort_3; otherwise skip it.
goals.rationale: I have a short planning horizon and low sleep discipline. Doing chores alone feels isolating and boring. If I can multitask it with socializing, great, but I won't prioritize it over the connection itself.

goals.description: Ignore the term project until tomorrow when I might feel 'ready' or pressured enough to start.
goals.rationale: The deadline is three days away, which feels like forever to an impulsive mind. Starting work now creates potential friction or stress, which I actively avoid. Procrastination bias is high, so delaying is the natural path of least resistance.

agent.last_reasoning: Advances my highest value (relationships) by relieving stress immediately. Avoids the friction of starting the project now and ignores the chore since it's not social; my short planning horizon prioritizes this instant connection over future consequences.
